summary: Iteration 219 — Anti-duplicate (delivery + offline_id + business-dup) verified end-to-end on the backend. 8/8 new DUP tests pass + 14/14 regression (captain + unassigned-delivery) tests pass. Frontend POS code review confirms the data-testid='delivery-company-order-id' input is wired correctly (renders only when a delivery company is selected, placeholder matches, value sent in all 4 order payloads). Live UI walkthrough was blocked by the long Maestro splash screen that the runner could not get past in 4.5s; verification done via code-grep and previous-iteration screenshots. Uncovered a real (latent) bug: the 30s content-fingerprint dedup in POST /api/orders does NOT include delivery_company_order_id, so two legitimately different delivery orders with same items/total within 30s get silently merged and the new ext_ref is lost.

critical.issue: Content fingerprint hash excludes delivery_company_order_id. Two distinct delivery orders (different ext_ref) but same branch+customer+items+total+order_type within 30s get merged: the second POST returns the FIRST order (with the old ext_ref) instead of creating a new order. This contradicts user's choice ب1 (block + warn) — silently merges instead. Reproduced in the failing iteration of the test before adding unique customer_phone per call: first POST ext_ref=A returned, second POST ext_ref=B returned existing order with ext_ref=A. Fix: include delivery_company_order_id (when present) in the fingerprint hash, OR check ext_ref dedup BEFORE the content fingerprint check (currently the order is reversed at lines 6061-6091 vs 6094+, but the content-fingerprint return at 6122 short-circuits without ever raising 409 for differing ext_refs).

minor.issue: OfflineOrder model requires `total` field but does not require `delivery_company_order_id`. Fine, but the duplicate-prevention message 'طلب التوصيل موجود مسبقاً (منع تكرار)' is good — consider including the existing order_number in the message for parity with online 409.
Already flagged in iter218 — still inserts captain_shift_links without tenant_id; relink workaround required. Not blocking this iteration.

integration_issues.flow: POS delivery order flow live UI test
integration_issues.issue: Could not exercise the delivery-company-order-id input end-to-end in Playwright because the Maestro EGP splash screen persists ~5-7s on each page (login + /pos) and my 4.5s waits were not enough; field appearance/disappearance verified instead via static code review of POS.js (lines 3782-3799). Code is correct: input renders only when `deliveryApp` is truthy (i.e., after company chosen), placeholder is 'رقم الطلب لدى شركة التوصيل (اختياري - يمنع التكرار)', value is included in all 4 order payloads (lines 1877, 2043, 2235, 2453).

test_results_backend.DUP-1 second_same_ext_ref_blocked_409: PASS — 409 with detail.code=='DUPLICATE_DELIVERY_ORDER' and Arabic message including existing order_number
test_results_backend.DUP-2 sync_same_ext_ref_returns_existing: PASS — /api/sync/orders returns success=true with the existing order_number and Arabic 'موجود مسبقاً'
test_results_backend.DUP-3 offline_id_idempotent: PASS — same offline_id returns same order_number even with different items

action_items: FIX (HIGH): Include delivery_company_order_id in the 30s content-fingerprint hash in server.py:6100-6104, OR re-check the external-ref guard against the 'existing_dup' returned by the fingerprint check before silently returning it. Otherwise two real delivery orders with same items/total within 30s are merged and one ext_ref is lost — defeating the whole point of choice ب1.
(Optional UX) Include existing order_number in the sync_routes.py duplicate-prevention message (مثل: 'طلب التوصيل #N موجود مسبقاً').
Carry over from iter218: add tenant_id to seed_captain_test_data.py inserts.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:6061-6091 (external-ref guard) and 6094-6126 (content-fingerprint guard) are in the right order, BUT the content-fingerprint guard's `existing_dup` short-circuit at 6122 does NOT verify that the existing order has the same delivery_company_order_id. Combine the two guards: if incoming ext_ref is set AND existing_dup.delivery_company_order_id != incoming ext_ref → either raise 409 or skip the fingerprint return.

Content fingerprint omits payment_method, delivery_company_order_id, and delivery_app. Even outside delivery, two consecutive distinct orders with same items can be merged. Recommend adding payment_method + delivery_company_order_id to the hash, and shortening the window to 5-10s for non-offline submissions.

/app/frontend/src/pages/POS.js is now 5148 lines — way over the 700-line guideline. Highly recommend extracting OrderTypeSelector, DeliveryCompanyPicker, CartSummary, PaymentDialog, etc. into separate files for maintainability and testability.
Same delivery_company_order_id logic is duplicated in 4 payload assembly sites in POS.js (lines 1877, 2043, 2235, 2453). Extract a buildOrderPayload(orderState) helper to eliminate drift risk.

OrderCreate model has BOTH `delivery_app`/`delivery_company`/`delivery_company_id`/`delivery_app_name`/`delivery_company_name` as separate optional fields — five fields representing essentially the same concept. Suggest consolidating into a single canonical `delivery_company_id` + denormalized `delivery_company_name` and deprecating the rest, or at minimum documenting which the backend treats as authoritative.
test_captain_my_shift_linked still returns a dict triggering PytestReturnNotNoneWarning — quick fix: change `return result` to `assert ... ; #` style.

/api/sync/orders OfflineOrder.items is `List[Any]` — no Pydantic validation on items shape. A typo in product_id won't fail validation; will fail later during pricing. Consider a typed OfflineOrderItem model.

success_rate.frontend: code-review only (static); UI live walkthrough not completed due to splash timing

test_credentials: admin@maestroegp.com/admin123 worked; backend BASE_URL read from /app/frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL
seed_data_creation: Ran /app/backend/seed_captain_test_data.py twice (once for regression, once to leave an OPEN shift behind for the main agent). End-of-run shift=warehouse-pos-hub-1 was OPEN after final seed — but DUP tests created several delivery orders against it, so it may show non-zero sales. Re-seed before any further test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend dup-prevention all GREEN. Main outstanding issue: content-fingerprint dedup at server.py:6094-6126 does NOT honour delivery_company_order_id — fix by including it in the hash OR re-validating the matched order's ext_ref before returning it. Frontend code review is sufficient (testid+placeholder+payload wiring confirmed); if live UI is needed, wait at least 7-8 seconds after navigating to /pos so the splash screen finishes before trying to click 'توصيل'. The test file /app/backend/tests/test_dup_delivery_iter219.py is idempotent and can be re-run after re-seeding.
rca of the issue: The bug I uncovered while iterating on the DUP-1 test (first POST returned different ext_ref than what I sent): server.py:6094-6126 builds a content fingerprint from (tenant, branch, cashier, customer_name, customer_phone, order_type, items_sig, discount). It deliberately excludes delivery_company_order_id to keep delivery-specific dedup logic in the earlier external-ref block (6061-6091). But the external-ref block ONLY fires when the new request has a non-empty ext_ref AND matches an existing one. If the EXISTING order in the DB has ext_ref=A and the NEW request has ext_ref=B (a brand-new delivery), the external-ref block does NOT fire (B has no match), but the content-fingerprint block DOES fire (same items/customer within 30s), and silently returns the existing order with ext_ref=A — the new ext_ref=B is dropped on the floor and the merchant thinks a duplicate was blocked when actually two real distinct delivery orders were collapsed into one. Mitigation in test: vary customer_phone per call (uuid). Mitigation in product: at the content-fingerprint match site, if incoming ext_ref is non-empty AND existing_dup.delivery_company_order_id != incoming ext_ref, then SKIP the merge and proceed to create a new order.
